mission.title: Install and Configure Opentofu on Kubernetes
mission.description: OpenTofu is an open-source tool that allows you to declaratively manage your cloud infrastructure safely and efficiently. By installing OpenTofu, you can leverage its capabilities to build, change, and version your infrastructure as code.

steps.title: Add the OpenTofu Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the OpenTofu Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add opentofu https://charts.krateo.io
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install OpenTofu using Helm
steps.description:
Install OpenTofu in a new namespace called 'opentofu'.
```bash
helm install opentofu opentofu/opentofu --namespace opentofu --create-namespace --version v1.11.5
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the OpenTofu pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace opentofu
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Edit the OpenTofu deployment to set resource limits for the containers.
```bash
kubectl edit deployment opentofu --namespace opentofu
# Add the following under spec.containers:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     cpu: "500m"
#     memory: "512Mi"
```

steps.title: Set up port forwarding for access
steps.description:
Forward the OpenTofu service port to your local machine for access.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/opentofu --namespace opentofu 8080:80
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the OpenTofu pods running in the 'opentofu' namespace, and you will be able to access the service through port 8080 on your local machine. Additionally, resource limits will be configured for the OpenTofu deployment.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add opentofu https://charts.krateo.io
helm repo update
helm install opentofu opentofu/opentofu --namespace opentofu --create-namespace --version v1.11.5

kubectl edit deployment opentofu --namespace opentofu
# Add the following under spec.containers:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     cpu: "500m"
#     memory: "512Mi"

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description: Uninstall the OpenTofu Helm release to remove the deployment from your cluster.

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description: Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) associated with OpenTofu.

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description: Delete the 'opentofu' namespace and verify that all resources have been cleaned up.

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description: Take a backup of the current OpenTofu configuration and resources before proceeding with the upgrade.

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description: Update the OpenTofu Helm repository and run the upgrade command to install the latest version.

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description: Check the status of the OpenTofu pods to ensure they are running the new version successfully.

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
This issue occurs when a pod fails to start repeatedly. Check the pod logs for errors using the command: 
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace opentofu
``` 
Fix the underlying issue causing the failure.

troubleshooting.title: Service not accessible
troubleshooting.description:
If the OpenTofu service is not accessible, verify that the port forwarding is set up correctly. Run: 
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace opentofu
``` 
Ensure the service is running and the correct ports are exposed.

troubleshooting.title: Helm upgrade fails with version conflict
troubleshooting.description:
If the Helm upgrade fails due to a version conflict, check the installed version with: 
```bash
helm list --namespace opentofu
``` 
You may need to specify a compatible version or rollback to a previous version.

troubleshooting.title: Resource limits causing OOMKilled
troubleshooting.description:
If pods are being terminated due to Out of Memory (OOM) errors, check the resource limits set on the deployment. Use: 
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace opentofu
``` 
Adjust the resource limits accordingly in the deployment.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Helm installed on your local machine.
